summary: Confidence gate rejection enforcement verified in bridge flow - rejected signals cannot reach open_position()

gate_invocation.action: Calls check_entry_gates() with signal and orderbook parameters

actions: Logs BRIDGE GATE REJECT with reason

successful_gate_path.condition: Only reached if _gate_ok is True (gate passed)

fix_mechanism.principle: Gate check precedes open_position() with mandatory continue on rejection
fix_mechanism.enforcement: The 'continue' statement at line 651 is UNCONDITIONAL within the rejection block, ensuring no open_position() call can occur after gate rejection
fix_mechanism.race_condition_prevention: _bridge_consumed_slugs tracks already-consumed slugs to prevent re-entry on same slug in same loop iteration

acceptance_criteria_verification: ✓ No live bridge path can call open_position() for a signal rejected by check_entry_gates()
✓ Rejected signals trigger 'continue' which skips open_position() entirely
✓ Sub-0.60 signals: check_entry_gates() rejects with 'confidence_too_low' reason and prevents open
✓ Valid >=0.60 signals: pass gate check and proceed to open_position()
✓ No files outside allowed scope modified (only bot_core.py reviewed)

audit_trail.rejection_logged: YES - line 644-646 logs BRIDGE GATE REJECT with reasons
audit_trail.breach_possible: NO - control flow makes it impossible for rejected signal to call open_position()

notes: This task was marked ARCHIVED, indicating it was previously completed. Verification confirms the fix is properly implemented. The confidence gate rejection enforcement is working as designed: rejected signals cannot reach open_position() due to the continue statement at line 651 which is unconditionally executed when gate fails.
